Level 510 == "'''Grayscale City"'''==
{{Class 1|color-h=0|color-s=100%|color-l=100%|header=Threat Index: Class|hexagon=▫️|class=Grayscale|safety=Not Completely Safe|security=Radiative Areas Are Present|entity=Minimal Entity Count}}
== ◽Description◽ == {{Blurimage|file=Level 510 - Pic 1.png|caption=First photograph of the level.}} Level 510 is the 511th level, from which its sublevel [[Level 510.5]] branches off. It is an abandoned, urban, medieval city made up of both big and small buildings made of wood, concrete, and rusting metal. Most of the structures are between one and three stories height. The windows and doors of the structures are rusty and broken. Posters with indistinguishable text are pasted to the buildings. Narrow, litter-laden streets lead to courtyards strewn with furniture and abandoned market stalls. A phenomena called "Grayscale Effect" is responsible for giving a complete achromatopsia when in the level, meaning everything in the level to be seen in black and white, this also affects cameras, as photos taken in the level are always captured in black and white. The atmosphere carries the smell of mold, stagnant water, and rust. {{Blurimage|file=Level 510 - Pic 2.png|caption=Photograph of a tower}} There are tall towers in certain areas. Though it's top is filled with fog, its base is accessible. The interior consists of rooms filled with dust, broken furniture, and remnants. No windows exist inside. The air inside is cold. The walls of the towers are covered with writings that say: "Save Society", "End Discrimination", "Help Me" and "Leave this place"The text were originally written in Pashto and Kanuri, which was later translated by [[M.E.G.]]. The market squares are large open areas surrounded by wooden stalls and makeshift canopies. Rusted shopping carts, empty crates, and torn fabrics are scattered throughout the area. Some stalls contain rotten food A huge warehouse with a corroded metal exterior is present. It is often seen behind the towers. Large equipment and defunct conveyor belts can be found inside. The machinery isn't working, but the sound is heard frequently.{{Blurimage|file=Level 510 - Pic 3.png|caption=Inside of the market stall.}} The inhabitants of the level are entities called The Citizens. There are plenty of evidence from books and diaries found in the level, of Ncipetherazi people once inhabiting the area. The Ncipetherazi are a group known for their culture, media, and military strength. Because of the Ncipetherazi’s religious myths about Clumps, we can assume that these creatures either inhabited or have been sighted in their territory before. {{Blurimage|file=Level 510 - Pic 4.png|caption=Photograph of a warehouse}} Some researchers believe that the architectural and symbolic patterns found throughout Level 510 were designed with deeper meaning, possibly reflecting the Ncipetherazi's worldview or cosmology. Repeating shapes like spirals, triangles, and incomplete circles are etched into the floors and walls of both residential and public structures, often near altars or communal gathering spots. These may have served as ritualistic markings or communication symbols. In the warehouse, having a name written "Blackscale" on it, several types of old and damaged firearms, along with large, non-functional cannons, were positioned near the far corners of the structure. Known recovered firearm types inc the Type-6 Boltrifle, N12 Compact Breaker, Ratch-77 Launcher, and [[Object 29|Kalthoff Rifles.]] == ◽Grayscale Effect◽ == Grayscale Effect gives temporary achromatopsia in all individuals within the level. This effect completely gets rid of color, resulting in a monochromatic perception of surroundings. Importantly, it doesn't only affect the human eye; digital devices, including cameras, video recorders, and image scanners, are also subject to this phenomenon. Any footage or photographs taken within the level are recorded in black and white, regardless of the actual color data. == ◽History◽ == {{Blurimage|file=Level 510 - Pic 5.png|caption=Painting of Ncipetherazi folklore's depiction of Clumps in the wall of a building.}}Though the precise year is not told accurate, the earliest known accounts of it was made in the early days of documented Backrooms research. Some among the Archivists claim to have found old journals describing an abandoned city, similar to Level 510, meaning that wanderers may have came to this level before its official documentation. The first documentation of Level 510, was published in 1759, February 8, written by George Madding. It explained the effect of the phenomenon called "Grayscale effect", inhabitants of the place and about the way of communication done by them. Some walls have been seen with Ncipetherazi depicted image of the Clumps. The reason behind this maybe because Clumps may have inhabited the level before. The absence of them now have been theorized with the help of books found in the level, written in German that Clump's organs had a high nutritional value. becoming a prime target of hunting. The decline of the civilization could've happened because of overhunting of Clumps or difficulty faced with exposure to the effects of the Grayscale phenomenon. It also could be more violent or environmental cause pointing to the damaged state of the towers and warehouses. == ◽Entities◽ == === '''The Citizens: "Homo Tenebris"''' === {{Blurimage|file=Cityzens.png|caption='''Picture of a giant next to Archivist George Madding and MEG Agent Treckstun Hiller'''}} Homo Tenebris or the Citizens are gaunt, tall human-like creatures wearing torn cloths. They are the only entities and inhabitants of the place. The skull protrudes through their head. They are not aggressive in nature. They are habitually found motionlessly waiting in alleyways, looking from windows, or walking in and out of structures. Do not try to interact with the Citizens since their actions are unpredictable. They form communities, interact within their own groups. They are highly social in nature, like human beings. They inhabit the buildings and towns. Their height ranges from 7 to 9 feet (2 to 2.7 meters), with some rare cases reaching 10 feet (3 meters). Weight varies significantly due to their malnourished appearance, but they are lighter than an average human due to their hollow bones. Their skin is ashen or pale-gray. Their skin stretched over their skulls, making their bone structure visible. No evidence of reproduction exists. Theories suggest they may be manifestations of a central intelligence, termed as The King or remnants of former humans. No signs of consumption have been observed meaning they do not require sustenance. The Citizens interact with their own language (termed as Citerian).Understood from a Czech book published about the level: Velcí muži šedé země (Great Men Of Gray Earth) They are also heard speaking a broken version of English, French and German. == ◽Colonies & Outposts◽ == There is no documented colonies or outposts established in the level, other than the '''Obelus'''. "''Obelus was never a name; they were a shadow in the system. By the time you realized they were watching, you were already part of their game.''" == ◽Entrances & Exits◽ == === Entrances === * Entering a rare hexagon hole in [[Level List|any level]] of the Backrooms will take you here. * A door labeled “Grayscale” in [[level 40]] leads here. * Noclipping in [[Level 95]] can bring one here abruptly. === Exits === * Sleeping in a haybale, in the warehouse leads to [[Level 193]]. * Noclipping in the tower can lead you to [[Level 11]]. * Climbing on a tree and jumping down leads to either [[Level 21]] or [[Level 83.1]]. * Randomly feeling insane leads to [[Level 58]]. * Jumping down staircase to the tower lead to [[Level 179|Level 179.]] * Accessing by entering a door with frosted glass lead to [[Level 510.5]]. * Unknown methods could lead to [[Level 511]]. * == ◽Footnotes◽ ==
